sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(100), created_at TIMESTAMP, duration INTERVAL);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, created_at, duration) VALUES (1, 'alice', TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01 08:00:00', INTERVAL '2 DAY');
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, created_at, duration) VALUES (2, NULL, TIMESTAMP '2023-01-02 12:00:00', INTERVAL '1 DAY');
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, created_at, duration) VALUES (3, 'bob', TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01 09:00:00', NULL);
SELECT ucase(name) AS upper_name, created_at + duration AS new_date FROM test_table WHERE created_at IS NOT NULL;
SELECT ucase(name) AS upper_name, COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE duration IS NOT NULL GROUP BY name HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
SELECT id, ucase(name) + ' joined on ' + CAST(created_at AS VARCHAR) AS joined_info FROM test_table WHERE name IS NOT NULL;
SELECT DISTINCT ucase(name) FROM test_table WHERE name IS NOT NULL AND LENGTH(name) > 3;
SELECT id, ucase(name) AS upper_name, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table t2 WHERE t2.created_at > test_table.created_at) AS future_count FROM test_table;
SELECT ucase(name) FROM test_table WHERE created_at + COALESCE(duration, INTERVAL '0 DAY') < TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01 15:00:00';
SELECT ucase(name) FROM test_table WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE created_at IS NOT NULL) ORDER BY ucase(name);
